title: Guard Killed in Shooting at San Diego Islamic Center, Mosque Leader Says
description: The police said the situation at the county’s largest mosque remained “active,” but did not confirm the report that at least one person had been killed.
author: Tim Arango and Neil Vigdor

title: Sandy Fire in Simi Valley Forces Immediate Evacuations
description: Officials ordered more than 23,000 to evacuate after a brush fire broke out in Simi Valley, Calif.
author: Soumya Karlamangla

title: American Hantavirus Patient Ordered to Stay in Quarantine Despite Plans to Leave
description: An American passenger from the hantavirus-infected cruise ship had wanted to depart an Omaha quarantine facility.
author: Sonia A. Rao

title: ICE Agent Charged in Shooting of a Venezuelan Immigrant in Minnesota
description: The agent is the second federal officer to face felony charges in Minnesota stemming from Operation Metro Surge, the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown.
author: Ernesto Londoño and Mitch Smith

title: Los Angeles’s Response to Altadena Fire Hampered by Chaos, Report Finds
description: A county-commissioned examination of the disaster concluded that its efforts were muddled and confused, but said that it had not discriminated against Black residents.
author: Jill Cowan

title: What is Trump’s $1.776 Billion ‘Anti-Weaponization’ Fund?
description: The Trump administration is creating a $1.8 billion fund to compensate people it says were wronged by the federal government, a group that could be largely made up of the president’s allies.
author: Andrew Duehren

title: Georgia Republicans Grasp for a Contender to Take On Democratic Sen. Jon Ossoff
description: On the eve of their primary, Republicans have grown nervous about their prospects in November against Mr. Ossoff, regarded as the most vulnerable Democratic senator in the fall midterms.
author: Tim Balk

title: Justice Dept. Announces $1.8 Billion Fund in Settling I.R.S. Suit
description: The dismissal appears to be part of the Trump administration’s effort to effectively settle the case, which stemmed from the leak of his tax returns in 2019.
author: Glenn Thrush, Andrew Duehren and Alan Feuer

title: Abortion Pill Lawsuit Leaves Trump in a Political Bind Ahead of the Midterms
description: Louisiana wants the Food and Drug Administration to curtail access to the medication. Doing so could cost Republicans at the polls.
author: Pam Belluck and Sheryl Gay Stolberg

title: Northwestern Names Mung Chiang as President After Tumult Over Protests
description: The previous president resigned after months of conflict over how the university handled protests. The new president, Mung Chiang, currently leads Purdue.
author: Stephanie Saul

title: Keisha Lance Bottoms Was Atlanta’s Mayor in the Pandemic. Can She Win the Governor’s Race?
description: Keisha Lance Bottoms is the Democrats’ front-runner as the Georgia primary for governor approaches. But her party rivals are trying to ensure she has to face a runoff.
author: Rick Rojas and Michelle Baruchman

title: He’s Been Impeached and Indicted. He Has a Chance in Texas’s Senate Race.
description: The fate of Ken Paxton, the conservative Texas firebrand, could decide whether Republicans keep control of the Senate.
author: Michael Kruse and Lauren McGaughy

title: Brookings Institution Report: Over 100,000 Family Separations in Trump Crackdown
description: The Brookings Institution suggests that federal statistics are an undercount because immigrant parents are not being asked about or not disclosing their American children.
author: Miriam Jordan and Jeff Adelson

title: In Massie’s Kentucky Primary, Fidelity to Trump Is on the Ballot
description: Representative Thomas Massie, the Republican who wears his rifts with President Trump as badges of honor, is battling Trump-backed challenger Ed Gallrein to keep his seat.
author: Robert Draper

title: Justice Dept. Announces $1.8 Billion Fund in Settling I.R.S. Suit
description: The dismissal appears to be part of the Trump administration’s effort to effectively settle the case, which stemmed from the leak of his tax returns in 2019.
author: Andrew Duehren, Glenn Thrush and Alan Feuer

title: Teenagers Arrested After Drive-By Shootings in Austin Injure at Least 4
description: The teenage boys were among three suspects taken into custody in connection with at least 12 shootings across the Texas capital. There was no apparent motive, the mayor said.
author: Edgar Sandoval

title: G.O.P. to Begin Debating Voting Maps in This State. Not Everyone Approves.
description: Under pressure from President Trump, the South Carolina legislature will consider new House districts this week. Some Republicans worry changes could backfire.
author: Eduardo Medina

title: Hegseth to Campaign for a Republican House Candidate
description: Defense Department officials typically stay away from partisan politics to maintain the military’s apolitical image.
author: Eric Schmitt

title: Two Military Jets Collide in Midair at Idaho Air Show
description: Video shared on social media showed the jets striking each other and spinning toward the ground. Four crew members safely ejected before the planes crashed, officials said.
author: Johnny Diaz and Edgar Sandoval

title: Trump’s Approval Sinks Amid Unpopular War, Darkening G.O.P. Prospects
description: With the midterms nearing, President Trump’s approval rating has hit a second-term low as voters question his handling of the economy, according to the latest New York Times/Siena poll.
author: Lisa Lerer, Ruth Igielnik and Camille Baker
